headlines.title: DOJ indicts Raúl Castro over fatal 1996 civilian planes’ shooting
headlines.description: The Justice Department indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on Wednesday in connection to the 1996 shooting of two civilian planes that killed four Cuban Americans.

headlines.title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa
headlines.description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: After 5 decades and 600 executions, these are the last words on Texas’ death row
headlines.description: An NBC News analysis of final statements from prisoners on Texas’ death row shows that some convey a readiness to die. Others are defiant. Many seek salvation.

headlines.title: Gallrein praises 'courageous' Trump in Kentucky primary victory speech
headlines.description: Navy veteran Ed Gallrein praised President Trump during his victory speech after defeating Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky's 4th Congressional District. Gallrein added that the winners are “the Republicans of Kentucky congressional district four and their families,” which he says he “will be a champion for in Washington."

headlines.title: Trump-endorsed Ed Gallrein unseats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky GOP primary
headlines.description: Former Navy SEAL Ed Gallrein has won the Republican primary in Kentucky’s 4th Congressional District over Rep. Thomas Massie.

headlines.title: Barney Frank, first openly gay member of Congress and Wall Street reformer, dies at 86
headlines.description: Former Rep. Barney Frank, who was the first openly gay member of Congress and known for the Dodd-Frank Act, died at age 86. He had entered hospice care at his home in Maine in April and is survived by his husband of 14 years, Jim Ready.

headlines.title: Trump’s $1.8B ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’ Draws Outrage
headlines.description: The Trump administration is defending what it calls its “anti-weaponization” fund, using $1.8 billion of taxpayer money to pay Trump allies who say they were wrongly prosecuted by the Biden administration, and is not ruling out compensating convicted Jan. 6 rioters. On Capitol Hill, Democrats grilled acting Attorney General and former Trump personal lawyer Todd Blanche. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: She paid an insurance company $99,000 to generate retirement income for life. Then it collapsed.
headlines.description: Risks are mounting in the life insurance industry, and experts are concerned state regulators are not protecting policyholders.

headlines.title: China and Russia sign agreements strengthening their ties after Xi-Putin meeting
headlines.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin to Beijing for a meeting one week after Xi met with President Donald Trump for a summit. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on how China is seeking to project itself as a stabilizing force on the world stage.

headlines.title: Virginia voters approve congressional redistricting amendment
headlines.description: NBC News projects that Virginia voters have approved redrawing the congressional district map. NBC News' Steve Kornacki provides analysis of the vote.

headlines.title: After 5 decades and 600 executions, these are the last words on Texas’ death row
headlines.description: An NBC News analysis of final statements from prisoners on Texas’ death row shows that some convey a readiness to die. Others are defiant. Many seek salvation.

headlines.description: The Justice Department indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on Wednesday in connection to the 1996 shooting of two civilian planes that killed four Cuban Americans.
